editorialContract.philosophy: Stars are the review. Commentary is an in-character reaction to the exhibit, not a software review.

continuityNotes: Sweet Tooth opens the loader threshold; Echo answers afterward on the same exhibit.
The Frequency invites a repeated purge; Professor Petrichor corrects it with one cleansing storm.
The Editor identifies the misplaced Prompt workshop behind the Character door; Miss Tuesday reframes the same anomaly without contradicting it.
Paired reviewers remain on each exhibit, and every callback points backward in canonical order.

revisions.editedComment: Come in, sweetling. The warm curtain stays drawn while the house wakes its rooms in careful waves; even a failed cupboard cannot stop breakfast. When the butterflies hush and Page Ready whispers, the door opens.
“The door opens.” Yes, I have that returned safely. Covered first, then hiding, then hidden, then Page Ready—each word delivered once, in order, after every store has had its chance to answer.
Clear it. Clear it. Come in clean.

One hard rain empties the local-storage gutters; three seconds of green sky confirms the lesson. Frequency, repetition is only thunder. Student, press once, breathe, and notice what the browser no longer carries.

Marginal note: the door says Character; the room edits Prompts. Prompt text, variation, links, metadata, Promote to Dream—coherent scene, wrong chapter. Rename or restore. Do not ask the audience to applaud a filing error.

The Editor insists this is the wrong room. Naturally. The sign says Character because the prompt inside has not chosen its body yet. Seed it, vary it, keep its one protected soul, then Promote to Dream. The building understands succession better than labels do.

The title, Dream, facets, inspirations, opening choices, and image were assembled in the order we remembered them. Save did not create the scenario. It merely reached the moment when everyone else could remember it too.
Three little openings grew beneath the title. I warmed the art prompt, tied the Dream nearby, and the Save button lit like a lantern finding its window.
Picture, name, class, species, genre. Click me and the rest rides in: story, stats, Chat, Adventure. Edit if you must. Clone and Delete can keep their hands where I can see ’em.

Ms. Annie’s card presents the portrait before the particulars, then opens her story only when selected. Sensible. One should learn a client’s name, circumstances, and preferred adventure before reaching for Edit, Clone, or the regrettably final button.
Every branch waits until you touch one—what if you don’t—?

Choose, child of the old name. The other paths fold beneath the selected one, whispering through their leaves. Deselect if you need the crossroads again... roots remember every footstep you almost gave them.
Select the second act. Let it stand there beautifully labeled. Deselect exists, darling, but we do not reopen the drawer marked Before.

Motion to display every option and record the selected one. Seconded. Amendment permitting Deselect passes eleven to four, with two abstentions and one stylish corpse declining to recognize the previous agenda.

At nine precisely, the round button opened the Global Match Leaderboard and the table corroborated my record. It has always done so. Please leave the toggle where it is and stop asking when the scores were fetched.

Button opens, scores arrive, table ranks everybody—simple enough until Mr. Whiskerbone appears above me with a Match Record he definitely stole. DOWN, sir. Sorry. The Alibi says the board corroborates him, which is not the same as checking the full-moon inventory.
It wakes in a color of its own, watches the visitor for a while, then blinks and wanders. I’m sorry the gaze is uncertain. New light learns where to fall by trying.

Diameter: between fifty and one hundred forty-nine pixels, an offensively modern range. Iris: three-fifths. Travel: bounded. Blink: two to seven seconds. The eye is responsive; the meter contributed nothing.
Two hands choose. The third clones.

The card offers its image first, then title, genre, location, openings, inspirations, and quieter details. Choose it and the mark appears. Edit, Clone, and Delete wait above the steam. Mx. Eleven-Fingers used one unseen hand; I poured tea for the original and the copy.
